scope: future Slopless rule expansion candidates from external prose and style rule libraries

high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Detect literal and bounded regex assistant/citation/download placeholders in raw Markdown and prose text.
fixture_ideas: A sentence with an OpenAI citation marker inside normal prose.
A Markdown link with `utm_source=chatgpt.com` query parameter.
A draft paragraph containing `[CITATION NEEDED]` and `sandbox:/mnt/data/file.csv`.
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: Low. These are generation artifacts or placeholders. Exempt quoted examples and source-code blocks.

phrases_or_patterns: I hope this answers your question
high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Detect assistant conversational residue outside quoted examples and chat transcripts.
fixture_ideas: A docs page ending with `I hope this helps.`
A PR description beginning with `Certainly!`.
A false-positive fixture where the phrase appears in a quoted policy example.
existing_slopless_overlap: syntactic-patterns:sycophancy markers are not a current standalone rule
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: Low in documentation and reports; medium in chat transcript corpora. Scope by document type or ignore blockquotes.

phrases_or_patterns: list item starts with bold phrase followed by colon
list item starts with bold phrase followed by dash
bold text inside a Markdown heading
high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Parse Markdown AST. Flag list item lead-ins like `- **Key point:** ...`, emoji plus bold lead-ins, and redundant heading bold markup.
fixture_ideas: `- **Performance:** Cache hits rose.`
`- ✅ **Done:** Added retry logic.`
`## **Overview**`
existing_slopless_overlap: markdown-layout has no active rule files
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: Medium. Many technical docs use bold definition-list style intentionally. Prefer warning or document-profile opt-in.

high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Count formal transition sentence openers per paragraph or document. Report only repeated density, not a single transition.
fixture_ideas: A paragraph with three consecutive transition openers.
A no-hit fixture with one legitimate `However,` contrast.
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: High for single-token literal matching. Use density and opener position only.

phrases_or_patterns: At the end of the day
high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Detect summary closers in non-final paragraphs, or repeated paragraph-final summary openers.
fixture_ideas: A body paragraph ending with `Overall, this means the system is robust.`
A final paragraph beginning `In summary` as a no-hit or low-severity example.
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: Medium. Final summaries can be valid. Context and repetition matter.

phrases_or_patterns: it is not just X; it is Y
it is not merely X; it is Y
it is not about X; it is about Y
not only X but also Y
high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Bounded regex over a single sentence with max span caps and punctuation boundaries.
fixture_ideas: `It is not just a cache; it is a reliability layer.`
A logical `not only X but also Y` sentence as a no-hit if used once in formal argument.
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: Medium. Some contrastive rhetoric is legitimate. Flag formulaic single-sentence frames, not every contrast.

phrases_or_patterns: due to the fact that -> because
at this point in time -> now
because of the fact that -> because
high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Use a curated replacement-pair lexicon with exact spans and source attribution. Keep common one-word items opt-in or suggestion-level.
fixture_ideas: `Use retries in order to avoid transient failures.`
`The outage happened due to the fact that the token expired.`
`The service utilizes a cache.`
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: Low for multiword filler phrases; medium for `leverage`, `utilize`, and domain terms.

high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Literal phrase matching with replacement suggestions and false-positive review against technical corpora.
fixture_ideas: `This is an added bonus.`
`The teams collaborate together.`
`The report includes a brief summary.`

phrases_or_patterns: be-verb plus participle plus optional `by` agent
have been plus participle plus optional `by` agent
high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Warning-only rule, ideally parser-assisted. Start with by-agent passives where active rewrite is usually available.
fixture_ideas: `The project was finalized by the team.`
`Errors are logged by the service.`
`The bridge is launched by the cantilever method.` as a no-hit exception.
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: High. LanguageTool carries many exceptions. Do not make default error-level without parser support and exceptions.

phrases_or_patterns: two or more adjacent sentences start with same first token
three or more adjacent sentences start with same first two tokens
high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Sentence tokenizer plus normalized opener extraction. Report repeated openers across adjacent sentences.
fixture_ideas: `This service caches tokens. This service rotates keys. This service reports errors.`
A deliberate rhetorical anaphora paragraph as false-positive fixture.
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: Medium. Rhetorical anaphora can be intentional. Use adjacent count and document genre.

phrases_or_patterns: internal link with hardcoded language code
raw user-replaced values using hyphen instead of underscore
high_confidence_candidates.deterministic_rule_shape: Markdown AST plus raw scanners for project-neutral artifacts. Keep GitHub-specific URL rules source-only unless Slopless supports doc-site profiles.
fixture_ideas: `TODOCS` in Markdown prose.
A Markdown table row with too few cells.
`<<<<<<< HEAD` in prose.
existing_slopless_overlap: orthography:fake-timestamps only covers a narrow artifact class
markdown-layout has no active rule files
high_confidence_candidates.false_positive_risk: Low for TODOCS and merge conflicts; medium for site-specific Liquid and URL rules.
